_comment: Hand-trimmed X versions of Bluesky originals from Apr 19-21 that were too long for X's 280-char limit. Built from content/social-briefs/x-mirror-blockers.json after de-duping against the 4 floor-crossing posts manually mirrored to X earlier today (Apr 26). Each entry: bskyUri (so we can mark as mirrored in state), text (≤255 chars so URL fits in remaining 25), slug (article URL). Run via mirror-queue-apply.ts.

queue.matchSubstring: Carney in-flight refreshment bill, Year 1
queue.text:
Carneys in-flight refreshment bill, Year 1 as PM:

$524,815 across 28 flights.
$21,000 on the 2-hr Washington flight alone.

Avg Canadian family of four: $16,297/yr on food.

Carneys plane catering = 32 of those budgets.

queue.matchSubstring: Ontario PCs defeated a bill to remove HST from groceries
queue.text:
Ontario PCs defeated a bill to cut HST from groceries on May 27.

Federal data: poorest decile pays 3.8% of income in GST, richest 2.1%. HST roughly doubles those.

The GST credit phases out by ~$54K. Middle class pays full HST.

queue.matchSubstring: How a federal bill becomes law in Canada
queue.text:
How a federal bill becomes law in Canada:

→ 1st reading (introduction)
→ 2nd reading (debate the principle)
→ Committee (clause-by-clause, testimony)
→ Report stage
→ 3rd reading
→ Other chamber
→ Royal Assent

queue.matchSubstring: Carney's $25B "sovereign wealth fund" is borrowed
queue.text:
Carney's $25B "sovereign wealth fund" is borrowed money.

Real SWFs — Norway, Singapore, the Gulf — run on surpluses. Canada is running a $66.9B deficit.

Like opening a new credit card with the others maxed out.

queue.matchSubstring: the RCMP signed a secret police-cooperation deal
queue.text:
On Carney's Jan 2026 Beijing trip, the RCMP signed a secret police-cooperation deal with China's Ministry of Public Security — the same body behind the illegal "police stations" here.

The RCMP won't release it.

queue.matchSubstring: Carney campaigned on building
queue.text:
Carney campaigned on building "the strongest economy in the G7."

Canada just posted two straight quarters of GDP decline — a technical recession, the first since COVID.

Mexico, hit by the same tariffs, grew 0.6%.

queue.matchSubstring: Designated terrorists have obtained Canadian entry
queue.text:
Designated terrorists have obtained Canadian entry:

A PFLP member convicted over a 1968 attack — granted residency 1987, removed only in 2013.
A UN-listed al-Qaeda financier — a citizen.
IRGC: 28 found inadmissible, 1 removed.

queue.matchSubstring: PM Mark Carney chaired Brookfield
queue.text:
PM Mark Carney chaired Brookfield's board until Jan 2025, weeks before becoming PM. He still holds ~US$6.8M in Brookfield options.

Parliament's Ethics Committee says his blind trust isn't enough — it wants divestment.

queue.matchSubstring: 100M for 200 Indian students
queue.text:
$100M for 200 Indian students (UofT pledge during PM Carney March 2026 India visit).
$138M/yr shortfall on First Nations water operations (PBO).
40 long-term boil-water advisories still active.

Two real numbers. Same year.

queue.matchSubstring: Canadian tankers: banned from BC
queue.text:
Canadian tankers: banned from BC north coast since 2019.
U.S. tankers from Alaska: still pass through.
Saudi tankers: still deliver crude to New Brunswick.

The Alberta-to-NB pipeline (Energy East) was cancelled in 2017.

queue.matchSubstring: When Quebec tried to leave Canada in 1995, the Crees voted first
queue.text:
When Quebec tried to leave Canada in 1995, the Crees voted first.

Oct 24, 1995: 96.3% of Crees voted No.
Oct 30, 1995: Quebec voted No by 0.58%.

2026: Treaty 6/7/8 chiefs are doing the same on Alberta.

queue.matchSubstring: The Snowbirds will fly their final show on October 11
queue.text:
The Snowbirds will fly their final show on October 11, 2026.

After 55 years and ~140M viewers, 431 Squadron stands down until at least the early 2030s.

Their replacement aircraft is a turboprop, not a jet.

queue.matchSubstring: The CRTC just tripled what Netflix owes Canada
queue.text:
The CRTC just tripled what Netflix owes Canada — from 5% to 15% of Canadian revenues.

Liberals call it a contribution, not a tax.
Streamers + US call it a tax.

Legally a regulatory charge. Functionally — on your bill — a tax.

queue.matchSubstring: Before 10 years of Liberals: Canada produced 2,393,890
queue.text:
Before 10 years of Liberals: Canada produced 2,393,890 vehicles in 2014.

After 10 years: 1,339,288 vehicles in 2024.

A 44% decline.

queue.matchSubstring: RCMP surveilled Tommy Douglas
queue.text:
RCMP surveilled Tommy Douglas — Medicare's founder — for 30+ years.

In 1995, CSIS told police land defenders at Ipperwash were armed. They were not.

CSIS later spied on Northern Gateway protesters + shared intel with Enbridge.

queue.matchSubstring: In 2014, the European Court of Justice struck down
queue.text:
In 2014, the EU's highest court struck down a near-identical metadata-retention regime.

The Court called it "particularly serious" interference with fundamental rights.

Canada is now proposing the same scheme.

queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-22 lets the Public Safety Minister order a telecom
queue.text:
Bill C-22 lets the Public Safety Minister order a telecom to build a surveillance capability into their service.

The telecom must comply. The telecom is legally barred from telling you.

Meta, Apple, EFF, and Geist all oppose this provision.

queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-22 creates new surveillance powers
queue.text:
Bill C-22 creates new surveillance powers + a 1-year metadata retention regime + secret capability orders.

The Privacy Commissioner has no statutory role in any of it.

Every prior lawful-access bill included one. C-22 doesn't.

queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-22 doesn't allow police to read your texts
queue.text:
Bill C-22 doesn't allow police to read your texts. It doesn't have to.

Metadata = who, when, where, what device. A year of that, on every Canadian.

Former NSA + CIA director Michael Hayden: "We kill people based on metadata."

queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-22 is at committee. The 12 Liberal MPs who put it there
queue.text:
Bill C-22 → committee. The 12 Liberal MPs who moved it:

Sponsor: Anandasangaree
Cabinet: Fraser, Sahota, Lattanzio
House Leader: MacKinnon
SECU: Duclos (chair), Acan, Dandurand, Housefather, Powlowski, Ramsay, Sodhi

queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-22 passed second reading April 20
queue.text:
Bill C-22 passed second reading April 20.

→ 1 year metadata retention
→ Secret order power for Public Safety
→ Lower access threshold

Opposed: Geist, EFF, Meta, Apple, US House Judiciary.

queue.bskyUri: May 15 — Canada net electricity importer Oct-Dec 2025 (Sonnet-reviewed accountability angle, hydro-exporter contrast)
queue.matchSubstring: Canada — a major hydro exporter — was a net electricity importer
queue.text: Canada — a major hydro exporter — was a net electricity importer for 3 consecutive months (Oct–Dec 2025). Dec 2025 US imports: 3.1M MWh, +79.3% YoY (StatsCan). Drivers: BC/Quebec drought + Ontario nuclear.

queue.matchSubstring: Canada's Defence Dept. pays Maritime Launch Services $20M
queue.text: Canada's Defence Dept. pays Maritime Launch Services $20M/yr for launch-pad access at Canso, NS — separate from MLS's $13,500/yr land-tenure fee. MLS 2025 filings: $14,980 revenue, $47M loss, no launches conducted.

queue.bskyUri: May 13 — Alberta vs Quebec, Sonnet-proofread precision rewrite
queue.matchSubstring: Alberta's independence petition cleared
queue.text: Alberta's independence petition cleared its threshold: ~302K signatures vs. 178K required. If certified, the legislature must respond with legislation or a referendum. Quebec's referendums (1980, 1995) were government-initiated.

queue.bskyUri: May 12 — Nomination-rules / non-citizens vote (new evergreen primer article)
queue.matchSubstring: Who can vote in a Canadian party
queue.text:
Who can vote in a Canadian party's nomination contest?

→ Liberal: anyone who "ordinarily lives in Canada"
→ CPC / NDP / Green: citizen or permanent resident
→ Bloc: no citizenship or residency requirement

queue.bskyUri: May 12 — Budget 2025 by-the-numbers (second post of the day)
queue.matchSubstring: Budget 2025, by the numbers
queue.text:
Budget 2025, by the numbers:

→ $60.6B in cuts over 4 years
→ 40,000 public service positions eliminated
→ CRA: -2,620 jobs (largest)
→ +$5.3B/yr to National Defence
→ Deficit target: $43.1B → $21.8B by 2028-29

queue.matchSubstring: Budget 2025 cuts 2,620 jobs
queue.text:
Budget 2025 cuts 2,620 jobs from the Canada Revenue Agency — the largest workforce reduction of any federal department in the $60B / 40,000-position plan.

CRA says automation will offset. PSAC warns audit capacity drops.

queue.bskyUri: Apr 19 — Bill C-9 vote split (will resolve at runtime via Bluesky search)
queue.matchSubstring: Bill C-9 passed the House 186
queue.text: Bill C-9 passed the House 186–137. Liberals + Bloc voted yes. Conservatives, NDP, and Greens voted no. The key issue: removing a decades-old religious speech defence.

queue.bskyUri: Apr 21 — Bill C-12 public-interest visa cancellation
queue.matchSubstring: can now cancel permanent resident visas
queue.text: The government can now cancel permanent resident visas, work permits, and study permits if it's deemed in the "public interest." That term is not defined in the law.

queue.bskyUri: Apr 21 — Sarnia mayor + local CPC president call for Gladu byelection
queue.matchSubstring: Sarnia's mayor and the local CPC
queue.text:
Sarnia's mayor and the local Conservative riding association president have both publicly called for Marilyn Gladu to face a byelection — matching her own January stance.

She has not committed.

queue.bskyUri: Apr 21 — d'Entremont 3 different reasons / one retracted
queue.matchSubstring: 5 days after crossing the floor
queue.text: In the 5 days after crossing the floor, Chris d'Entremont gave at least 3 different reasons. One had to be retracted. One cited the federal budget — which hadn't been tabled yet.

queue.bskyUri: Apr 21 — Ma "truly a Conservative" Christmas party quote
queue.matchSubstring: I attended the Conservative Christmas party
queue.text:
"The night I attended the Conservative Christmas party, I was truly a Conservative member… I had not made a decision at that point."

— Michael Ma, on the morning he crossed to the Liberals.
queue.imageQuote: The night I attended the Conservative Christmas party, I was truly a Conservative member… I had not made a decision at that point.
queue.imageAttrib: Michael Ma, the morning he crossed to the Liberals
queue.imageContext: Markham–Unionville MP. Crossed Dec 11, 2025 — 9 days after a Hansard speech calling the Liberals "team feudalism."

queue.bskyUri: Apr 21 — Jeneroux named Special Advisor + Carney trip
queue.matchSubstring: named Special Advisor to the PM
queue.text: Weeks after crossing, Matt Jeneroux was named Special Advisor to the PM on Economic and Security Partnerships, and joined Mark Carney on a trip to India, Japan, and Australia.

skip_as_duplicate.matchSubstring: MP Marilyn Gladu said constituents "deserve a chance to have a redo"
skip_as_duplicate.reason: Same Gladu Jan-11-quote angle as today's manual X post
Same Ma team-feudalism angle as today's manual X post

skip_as_duplicate.matchSubstring: Bailey's Law would make killing an intimate partner
skip_as_duplicate.reason: Bailey's Law (1st-degree murder framing) covered by today's manual X post
Bailey's Law (committee/PMB framing) covered by today's manual X post

skip_as_duplicate.matchSubstring: won by 533 votes as a CPC in Apr 2025
skip_as_duplicate.reason: Same d'Entremont 533-votes angle as today's manual X post

skip_as_duplicate.matchSubstring: Nov 6, 2025: MP Matt Jeneroux announced he was resigning
skip_as_duplicate.reason: Same Jeneroux resign-then-cross arc as today's manual X post
